article.body_preview:
DHAKA - Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina yesterday asked the ministries concerned to come up with a roadmap to tackle inflation and power crisis, the biggest sources of people's sufferings.

"Inflation can't be allowed to rise any further," Planning Minister MA Mannan quoted the prime minister as saying at a meeting of the Executive Committee of the National Economic Council (Ecnec).

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Asked why the prices of certain products were rising in Bangladesh even though they were getting cheaper in the international market, Mannan said this happened because of the dollar crisis.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article attributes higher domestic prices (despite cheaper international prices) to a 'dollar crisis', explicitly linking exchange-rate effects to higher import/ buying prices at the national level.

verification.quote_best_match: Asked why the prices of certain products were rising in Bangladesh even though they were getting cheaper in the international market, Mannan said this happened because of the dollar crisis.
verification.judge_reasoning: The article directly quotes Mannan saying rising domestic prices happened 'because of the dollar crisis' and explains the taka's loss of value raises the 'actual buying price,' explicitly linking the dollar crisis (exchange-rate effect) to higher import/domestic prices for Bangladesh.
